description: WARZONE 5: World Simulation Engine - Time, Diplomacy, and Dynamic Events

techContext.typecheckCommand: mypy --strict src/contexts/world && npm --prefix frontend run type-check
techContext.conventions: Hexagonal Architecture. Domain Events. Result pattern. shadcn/ui + Tailwind only.

userStories.title: Domain - WorldCalendar Value Object
userStories.description: Create WorldCalendar value object with year, month, day, era_name fields.
acceptanceCriteria: Fields: year (int, min=1), month (int, min=1, max=months_per_year), day (int, min=1, max=days_per_month), era_name (str), days_per_month (int=30), months_per_year (int=12)
Method: advance(days: int) -> Result[WorldCalendar, ValueError] (returns new instance, returns Err(ValueError) if days < 0)
Method: format() -> str (e.g., 'Year 1042, Month 3, Day 15 - Third Age')
Class method: validate(year, month, day, ...) -> Result[WorldCalendar, ValueError] (returns Err(ValueError) for invalid values)
- advance within month, month rollover, year rollover
- invalid month (0, 13) returns Err(ValueError) on creation
- invalid day (0, 32) returns Err(ValueError) on creation

userStories.title: Domain - Calendar Integration

acceptanceCriteria: Replace world_time: datetime field with calendar: WorldCalendar
Update advance_time() method to use calendar.advance()
Add class method: from_datetime_world_time(dt: datetime) for migration

userStories.title: API - Calendar Router
userStories.description: Create API endpoints for calendar operations.
acceptanceCriteria: Router prefix: /calendar (NO /api prefix in decorator - it's added by include_router)

userStories.description: Create React component to display and control calendar.

Use shadcn/ui Card component for container
Display: formatted date (large text, aria-live='polite'), era name (subtitle), Advance buttons (1 day, 7 days, 30 days)
Use TanStack Query for data fetching
Handle loading, error, and success states
Error boundary: catch and display API errors with retry button
- All buttons have aria-label describing action (e.g., 'Advance time by 1 day')
- Keyboard navigation: Tab through buttons, Enter/Space to activate
- Focus visible on all interactive elements
- Date change announced via aria-live region
- useAdvanceCalendar(worldId) mutation hook with optimistic update rollback
E2E test: verify component renders, buttons work, error state displays

acceptanceCriteria: Follow existing MemoryTimeline.tsx pattern for styling (reference: frontend/src/features/characters/components/MemoryTimeline.tsx)
Vertical timeline with date grouping (use date from API, formatted client-side)
Each event shows: title, date, type badge, impact scope badge, description (max 200 chars with expand)

Invariant validation: set_status returns Err(ValueError) if creates conflict
(e.g., A declares ALLIED but B has AT_WAR)

userStories.title: API - Diplomacy Router
userStories.description: Create API endpoints for diplomacy operations.

userStories.title: Frontend - Diplomacy Matrix Grid
userStories.description: Create visual grid showing faction relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: Data grid: rows = factions, columns = factions, cells = relationship color
Diagonal cells (self-relation) shown as gray with '-' character
Color mapping: ALLIED='#22c55e', FRIENDLY='#84cc16', NEUTRAL='#eab308', COLD='#f97316', HOSTILE='#ef4444', AT_WAR='#dc2626'
Hover cell: tooltip shows faction names and status label
Click cell opens shadcn/ui Dialog to edit relation
Dialog contains: faction A name, faction B name, current status, status dropdown, save/cancel buttons
Error handling: Display toast on save failure, optimistic update with rollback
- Grid has role='grid' with aria-label='Faction diplomacy matrix'
- Each cell has role='gridcell' with aria-label='Relation between {A} and {B}: {status}'
- Keyboard: Arrow keys navigate cells, Enter opens dialog
- Dialog traps focus and has aria-modal='true'
Use shadcn/ui Table (or custom grid), Dialog, Select, Tooltip components
Handle loading state with skeleton grid (same dimensions)
E2E test: verify grid renders, tooltip shows, click cell opens dialog, save works

userStories.title: Domain - FactionIntent Entity
userStories.description: Create entity representing a faction's intended action.
acceptanceCriteria: Create IntentType enum: EXPAND, ATTACK, DEFEND, ALLY, TRADE, RECOVER, CONSOLIDATE
- target_id: str | None (faction_id or location_id depending on type)

userStories.title: Application - FactionIntentGenerator
userStories.description: Create service to generate faction intents based on world state (rule-based).
acceptanceCriteria: Method: generate_intents(faction: Faction, world: WorldState, diplomacy: DiplomacyMatrix) -> list[FactionIntent]
Rule-based decision logic (evaluated in order, first match wins for each category):
1. If wealth < 20: RECOVER intent (priority 9, narrative: 'Recover economic stability')
2. If has_enemies (diplomacy.get_enemies) and strongest_enemy.military < faction.military * 0.5: ATTACK intent targeting that enemy (priority 7)
3. If has_no_allies (len(diplomacy.get_allies) == 0) and wealth > 40: ALLY intent with wealthiest neutral faction (priority 6)
4. If len(territories) < 3 and wealth > 50: EXPAND intent targeting adjacent unclaimed location (priority 5)
5. If military > 80 and has_enemies: DEFEND intent on border territory (priority 4)
6. Default: CONSOLIDATE intent (priority 1, narrative: 'Focus on internal affairs')
- If faction has status == COLLAPSED: return empty list
- If no valid target for EXPAND/ATTACK: skip that intent
- If ALLY target has conflicting relations: skip that intent
Return max 3 intents per faction, sorted by priority (descending)
Create tests with mock Faction, WorldState, and DiplomacyMatrix
Test scenarios: critical resources, no enemies, no territories available, collapsed faction

userStories.title: Domain - SimulationTick Value Object
userStories.description: Create value object to record simulation step results.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ResourceChanges dataclass: wealth_delta, military_delta, influence_delta
Create DiplomacyChange dataclass: faction_a, faction_b, status_before, status_after

userStories.title: Application - Basic Simulation Tick
userStories.description: Create basic simulation service that advances calendar and generates faction intents.
acceptanceCriteria: Constructor: __init__(self, world_repo: WorldStateRepository, faction_repo: FactionRepository, intent_generator: FactionIntentGenerator)
Method: advance_simulation(world_id: str, days: int = 1) -> Result[SimulationTick, SimulationError]
1. Validate days (1-365), return Err(SimulationError.INVALID_DAYS) if invalid
2. Load WorldState from repository, return Err(SimulationError.WORLD_NOT_FOUND) if not found
5. Load all factions for this world
7. For each active faction: generate intents using FactionIntentGenerator
8. Create SimulationTick with results (no changes applied to world)
Create SimulationError enum in src/core/errors.py: WORLD_NOT_FOUND, INVALID_DAYS, REPOSITORY_ERROR
Create tests with mock repositories covering:

userStories.title: Application - Intent Resolution
userStories.description: Add intent resolution logic to simulation service.
acceptanceCriteria: Add method: _resolve_intents(intents: list[FactionIntent], world: WorldState) -> ResolutionResult
- ATTACK: Reduces attacker and defender military (-5 each), may change territory ownership
- EXPAND: Requires wealth >= 20, reduces wealth by 10, adds territory if available
- ALLY: Creates bilateral ALLIED status if target accepts (target has no conflicts)
- RECOVER: Increases wealth by +5 if below 50
- DEFEND: Increases military by +3 if below 80
Handle conflicts: two factions attacking same target
Create ResolutionResult dataclass with changes to apply

userStories.title: Application - Full Simulation Integration
userStories.description: Complete simulation service with snapshots, events, and side effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Add constructor dependency: snapshot_service: SnapshotService, rumor_service: RumorPropagationService, sanity_checker: SimulationSanityChecker
Add method: commit_simulation(world_id: str, days: int = 1) -> Result[SimulationTick, SimulationError]
1. Validate inputs, return Err if invalid
3. Run advance_simulation (get tick), propagate Err if fails
- WAR event if ATTACK resulted in territory change
- ALLIANCE event if ALLY intent succeeded
- POLITICAL event for faction status changes
7. Run SanityChecker, log warnings via structlog (don't block commit)
- If save fails: attempt rollback to snapshot, return Err(SimulationError.SAVE_FAILED)
Add simulation history tracking (in-memory dict, max 100 entries per world)
Update tests covering: successful commit, snapshot failure, save failure with rollback

userStories.title: API - Simulation Router
userStories.description: Create API endpoints for simulation operations.
acceptanceCriteria: - SimulateRequest: days (int, ge=1, le=365, description='Number of days to simulate')
- SimulationTickResponse: tick_id, world_id, calendar_before, calendar_after, days_advanced, events_generated (list), resource_changes (dict), diplomacy_changes (list), created_at
- 503: Simulation locked (another simulation in progress for this world)
BackgroundTasks: Use for commit with days > 30, return 202 Accepted with status URL
Rate limiting: Max 10 commits per minute per world_id (in-memory tracking)
Create tests/api/routers/test_simulation.py covering all endpoints and errors

Add method: get_age(current_calendar: WorldCalendar) -> int | None
- Returns None if birth_date is None
- Returns age in years (simplified: year difference)
Add field: death_date: WorldCalendar | None = None
All new fields nullable for backward compatibility

userStories.description: Add location tracking to Character entity.

Add method: move_to(location_id: str) -> Character (returns new instance)

userStories.title: Domain - CharacterReaction Value Object
userStories.description: Create value object for character reactions to events.
acceptanceCriteria: Create ReactionType enum: OBSERVE, FLEE, INVESTIGATE, IGNORE, CELEBRATE, MOURN, PROTEST

userStories.title: Application - Character Reactor
userStories.description: Create service for character reactions to world events.
acceptanceCriteria: Method: react_to_event(character: Character, event: HistoryEvent, world: WorldState) -> CharacterReaction
Intensity calculation formula: base_intensity + modifiers (range 1-10, clamped)
- base_intensity by impact_scope: LOCAL=3, REGIONAL=5, GLOBAL=7
1. If event.event_type == WAR and 'pacifist' in character.traits: PROTEST
2. If event.event_type == DEATH and event.affected_character_id in character.relationships: MOURN
3. If event.event_type == TRADE and 'merchant' in character.traits: CELEBRATE
4. If event.event_type == WAR and character.current_location in event.affected_locations: FLEE
Integration: Call memory_service.create_memory(character.id, event.description, intensity) if reaction_type != IGNORE
Create tests with mock Character, HistoryEvent, WorldState covering all reaction types

userStories.title: Frontend - Character Location Display

acceptanceCriteria: For each location node, show character avatars as children (max 5 visible, '+N more' for overflow)

Avatar shows: name tooltip on hover, status indicator ring (green=alive, gray=deceased)
Click avatar opens CharacterDetailDialog (reuse existing if available, otherwise minimal dialog with name, status, location)
Add filter toggle button: 'Show Characters' / 'Hide Characters' with icon
Handle: characters without location (show in 'Unknown Location' category at bottom)
- Tree structure uses role='tree' with aria-label='World locations and characters'
- Location nodes have role='treeitem' with aria-expanded
- Character avatars have role='img' with aria-label='{character name}, {status}'
- Keyboard: Arrow keys navigate, Enter expands/collapses
E2E test: verify characters appear under correct locations, filter works, dialog opens

userStories.title: Application - Sanity Checker
userStories.description: Create service to validate world state consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: Create SanityViolation dataclass: rule_name, severity, message, affected_ids
1. dead_character_has_location: ERROR if deceased character has current_location_id
2. faction_no_influence_has_territories: WARNING if faction.influence == 0 and has territories
3. circular_location_hierarchy: ERROR if location parent chain has cycles
4. orphaned_entities: WARNING if entity references non-existent parent
5. duplicate_faction_relations: ERROR if faction has duplicate relation entries
Method: check_and_raise(world) -> None (raises on ERROR severity)
Create tests with various violation scenarios

2. Click Preview 7 days, verify preview shows
